config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat masyarakat kita, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran yang sebenarnya tidak adil. Yang ideal adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menempatkan masyarakat selalu di atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, even though social stability and collective interests matter a great deal. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people emphasize the group, because public order, family duty, and common goals are deeply valued, but I also think that without clear limits, “society first” can become an excuse for ignoring unfair treatment of ordinary people. A healthy society is not one that simply suppresses individual rights for the majority, but one that protects basic rights while still asking individuals to contribute to the common good. In that sense, the real priority should be balance, not unconditional supremacy of either side.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya di Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, menutup kritik, atau membenarkan keputusan yang tidak adil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru hak individu itu batas agar mayoritas dan negara tidak bertindak semena-mena. Yang paling masuk akal menurut saya adalah menempatkan hak dasar sebagai pagar utama, lalu mencari keseimbangan dengan kepentingan sosial yang benar-benar sah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value collective goals and social stability, but I have also seen how easily “for the good of society” can be used to silence complaints or excuse unfair treatment. If basic rights are treated as secondary, then the individual becomes too weak to resist abuse by the majority or by authority. The better principle is to protect fundamental rights first, while still expecting people to act responsibly toward the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, membungkam kritik, atau melegalkan kebijakan yang sebenarnya tidak adil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru hak individu adalah pagar supaya mayoritas dan negara tidak semena-mena. Jadi, yang benar bukan menomorsatukan masyarakat secara mutlak, melainkan melindungi hak dasar dulu lalu menyeimbangkannya dengan kebutuhan publik yang benar-benar sah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because once basic rights are treated as secondary, people become too vulnerable to abuse by both the majority and the authorities. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why collective interests and social stability are emphasized, but I have also seen how easily “for the good of society” can be used to silence ordinary people or excuse unfair decisions. In my view, a society is only genuinely strong when it protects fundamental rights first and then asks individuals to contribute responsibly to the common good. So I would not accept unconditional supremacy of society over the individual; balance is necessary, but rights must be the baseline.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
